source.title: Rapid response to fast viral evolution using AlphaFold 3-assisted topological deep learning

initial_request.research_problem: Fast viral evolution outpaces experimental DMS and 3D structure determination, hindering timely forecasting of dominant variants.
initial_request.target_object: SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 binding free energy changes upon mutation
initial_request.known_context: Experimental DMS and 3D structures are bottlenecks for rapid response to viral evolution. The earliest experimental DMS on SARS-CoV-2 RBD mutations was not available until September 2020, nine months after the outbreak.
constraints: High-quality 3D structures are required for TDL-based predictions, but are often delayed during pandemics.
input_data: Four experimental SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets (6M0J-RBD-1, 6M0J-RBD-2, 7T9L-RBD, 7XB0-RBD)
quantifiable_goals: Maintain predictive performance (PCC, RMSE) when substituting experimental structures with AF3-predicted structures
Extend to new variants (e.g., HK.3) with acceptable accuracy

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental DMS and 3D structures are bottlenecks for rapid response to viral evolution.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can AF3-predicted structures substitute experimental structures for TDL-based DMS prediction without significant performance loss?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AF3-predicted structures can substitute experimental structures for TDL-based DMS prediction.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose an AF3-assisted multi-task topological Laplacian (MT-TopLap) strategy to bypass experimental structure determination.
evolution_trajectory.observation: AF3 can predict 3D structures of RBD-ACE2 complexes, enabling feature extraction without experimental structures.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design the MT-TopLap model integrating features from AF3 structures.

evidence.quote_or_span: the DMS experiments are typically a bottleneck in response to fast evolving viruses
the earliest experimental DMS on the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein RBD mutations was not available until September 2020, nine months after the outbreak
We propose an AlphaFold 3 (AF3)-assisted multi-task topological Laplacian (MT-TopLap) strategy to address this need.

we leverage AF3's predicted structures to extract persistent Laplacian features. As such, we can speed up in-silico DMS predictions by bypassing the experimental determination of PPI complex structures.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated; need a model that can use AF3 structures for DMS prediction.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to integrate topological, auxiliary, and sequence features for multi-task learning of BFE changes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Integrating persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained transformer features in a multi-task deep learning framework will accurately predict BFE changes upon mutation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design MT-TopLap with persistent Laplacian features from AF3 structures, auxiliary features, and ESM-2 transformer embeddings, trained on SKEMPI 2.0 with multi-task learning.

parameters.features: persistent Laplacian (0D: 1872, higher-D: 420), auxiliary, ESM-2 transformer

evolution_trajectory.observation: Model architecture defined; features extracted from AF3-generated complexes.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate the model on four experimental DMS datasets.

evidence.quote_or_span: MT-TopLap integrates persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained transformer features to predict BFE changes upon RBD DMS mutations.

SKEMPI 2.0 is used as training data with a multi-task deep learning approach that simultaneously learns single-site mutational patterns with experimental BFE changes and DMS profiles with experimental enrichment ratios.

the AF3 generated 3D SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 complex is used to generate persistent Laplacian features and auxiliary features. Persistent Laplacian features provide a topological representation, using element-and site-specific atom sets to simplify the structural complexity of protein–protein complexes and encode vital biological information into topological invariants.

The ESM-2 pre-trained transformer is used to convert amino acid sequence of the PPI into a pre-trained transformer embedding. All these features are concatenated and fed into MT-TopLap to predict the BFE change upon mutation.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MT-TopLap designed; need to test on SARS-CoV-2 DMS data.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does AF3-assisted MT-TopLap perform compared to using experimental structures on multiple DMS datasets?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap will maintain performance close to that using experimental structures.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate on four experimental RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets (6M0J-RBD-1, 6M0J-RBD-2, 7T9L-RBD, 7XB0-RBD) using both AF3 and PDB structures with 10-fold cross-validation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieved PCC and RMSE values close to those using PDB structures across all datasets.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Quantify the performance difference between AF3 and experimental structures.

evidence.quote_or_span: We validate our AF3-assisted MT-TopLap approach with four experimental RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets of SARS-CoV-2 and its variants

we first collected four experimental RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets, each paired with an experimental 3D SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 complex structure. Meanwhile, we also create alternative AF3 3D RBD-ACE2 complex structures from the AlphaFold 3 Server.

we perform dataset-level 10-fold cross-validation on four experimental SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets. The learning rate is set to 0.0001 and 500 epochs are used for the pre-training while 200 epochs are used for the validation step.
The four experimental DMS datasets comprise of two datasets from the original SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMSs (6M0J-RBD-1, 6M0J-RBD-2) [7, 45], and datasets from the BA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validation completed; need to assess the impact of using AF3 structures.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the quantitative performance drop when using AF3 structures instead of experimental ones?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The performance drop will be minimal, justifying the use of AF3 structures for rapid response.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compute average PCC decrease and RMSE increase across the four datasets.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Average PCC decreased by 1.1% and RMSE increased by 9.3% compared to using PDB structures.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Highlight best-performing dataset and consider fine-tuning for new variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap maintains strong performance in predicting BFE changes upon DMS mutations, with an average decrease of only 1.1% in Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) and an average increase of only 9.3% in RMSE, compared to the use of experimental RBD-ACE2 structures.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap only displayed an average decrease of 1.1% in PCC when compared to using Protein Data Bank (PDB) structures.
the four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets displayed an average of 9.3% increase in RMSE when validated with AF3-assisted MT-TopLap.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Overall performance quantified; best dataset identified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which dataset shows the best performance with AF3 structures?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The 7XB0-RBD dataset will yield the highest PCC and lowest RMSE.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Report best performance on 7XB0-RBD with AF3 structures.
evolution_trajectory.observation: PCC of 0.968 and RMSE of 0.338 kcal/mol, only 0.6% lower PCC than using PDB structures.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Extend to new variant HK.3 via transfer learning.

evidence.quote_or_span: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap also achieved the best performance in the validation test for 7XB0-RBD with a PCC of 0.968, only 0.6% lower than the performance using PDB complexes.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap's predicted BFE changes for 7XB0-RBD obtained the lowest RMSE of 0.338 kcal/mol

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Model validated on existing variants; need to adapt to emerging variant HK.3.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can AF3-assisted MT-TopLap be fine-tuned with limited experimental DMS data for a new variant?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Transfer learning with latest experimental DMS data will enable accurate prediction for HK.3.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use transfer learning to fine-tune AF3-assisted MT-TopLap with HK.3 DMS data and AF3-predicted structure.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Model updated with HK.3 data; ready for validation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate fine-tuned model on HK.3 DMS.

evidence.quote_or_span: To enhance AF3-assisted MT-TopLap's capabilities using experimental DMS data, we use transfer learning to update AF3-assisted MT-TopLap by training it with latest experimental DMS data.
we use the AlphaFold Server to predict the 3D structure of HK.3 variant RBD-ACE2 complex.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Fine-tuned model for HK.3; need to assess prediction accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How well does the fine-tuned model predict BFE changes for HK.3 mutations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The fine-tuned model will achieve acceptable accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform 10-fold cross-validation on HK.3 DMS data.

parameters.pre-training: SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets and S8338 dataset

evolution_trajectory.observation: PCC of 0.81 and RMSE of 1.10 achieved.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze predictions by structural regions and mutation types.

evidence.quote_or_span: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves a PCC of 0.81 and RMSE of 1.10 for this fine-tuning validation.
The predicted BFE changes are obtained by performing a 10-fold cross-validation after MT-TopLap has been pre-trained with the SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets and the S8338 dataset.
The patterns of the predicted DMS is observed to resemble the patterns of the converted BFE changes with a PCC of 0.81 and an RMSE of 1.10.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: HK.3 validation completed; further analysis of predictions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do predictions vary across structural regions and mutation types?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Predictions will be more accurate for binding interface mutations and will align with experimental patterns across amino acid types.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze BFE changes by structural regions (support, core, rim) and by mutation types (20 amino acid types, charged, polar, hydrophobic).
evolution_trajectory.observation: High PCCs for binding interface regions (0.947, 0.943, 0.931); predicted BFE changes by mutation types align with experimental data, though predicted variance is lower.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Track variant evolution using predicted BFE changes.

evidence.quote_or_span: The combined prediction results for different residue region types for four AF3-assisted SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets according to Fig. 1(a) with PCCs of 0.947, 0.943, and 0.931 for the binding interfaces: support, core, and rim respectively.
the variance of the predicted BFE changes are generally lower than the variance of the experimental values

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Regional and mutation type analysis done; apply to variant tracking.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can predicted BFE changes explain the emergence of new variants like KP.3?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Predicted BFE changes will reflect mutational effects that drive variant dominance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze BFE changes for key mutations in KP.3 (Q493E, L455S, F456L).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Q493E alone decreases BFE, but combined with L455S and F456L reverses effect, increasing BFE by 0.455 kcal/mol.

evidence.quote_or_span: in the current KP.3 variant, when Q493E is combined with L455S and F456L mutations, the effect has reversed thus promoting an increase in BFE changes
This is consistent with Figure 4 where a positive BFE change of $0.455\mathrm{kcal / mol}$ due to Q493E mutation led to the KP.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: All analyses completed; need to summarize overall performance and limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does AF3-assisted MT-TopLap provide a robust and rapid alternative to experimental structure-based prediction?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves robust performance with minimal deviation from experimental structures and extends to new variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that AF3-assisted MT-TopLap is effective, but note the limitation of requiring high-quality structures.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Robust performance on four DMS datasets (avg. 1.1% PCC drop, 9.3% RMSE increase) and successful extension to HK.3 (PCC 0.81). Limitation: still requires 3D structures, which may be delayed.

evidence.quote_or_span: With 3D PPI complexes generated by AF3, AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves robust predictive performance, showing minimal deviation from results obtained using high-quality experimental 3D structures.
Our model's success extends to new variants, including the SARS-CoV-2 HK.3 variant, where it achieved a Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.81.
MT-TopLap requires the 3D structures of new viral variants to achieve accurate predictions. Such structures are typically not available until many months later during pandemics.

AF3-assisted MT-TopLap maintains strong performance in predicting BFE changes upon DMS mutations, with an average decrease of only 1.1% in Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) and an average increase of only 9.3% in RMSE, compared to the use of experimental RBD-ACE2 structures.

validation_methods: 10-fold cross-validation on four experimental DMS datasets
Comparison of AF3-assisted vs. PDB structure-based predictions
Transfer learning and validation on HK.3 variant
success_verification.final_conclusion: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap maintains robust predictive performance with only a 1.1% average PCC decrease and 9.3% RMSE increase compared to experimental structures, and successfully extends to the HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81, demonstrating its potential for rapid response to viral evolution.
limitations: TDL requires high-quality 3D structures, which may still be delayed during pandemics.
Predicted BFE changes have lower variance than experimental values.
Performance on HK.3 (PCC 0.81) is lower than on training variants.
